rationale: This reaction shows the decarbonylation of a simple, saturated aliphatic aldehyde (heptanal) to its corresponding alkane (heptane) using Wilkinson's catalyst. This is a classic, textbook example of the Tsuji-Wilkinson decarbonylation, clearly demonstrating the conversion of an aldehyde functional group (-CHO) to a hydrogen atom (-H) with the extrusion of carbon monoxide. The substrate is acyclic and lacks other reactive functional groups, making it an unambiguous representation of this specific transformation.
collision_avoidance_note: The substrate is a simple aliphatic aldehyde, avoiding aromatic rings which could be confused with other transition-metal catalyzed C-H activation or cross-coupling reactions. It also avoids acyl halides, which are less common substrates and could overlap with other named reactions involving acid derivatives. The simplicity focuses squarely on the aldehyde-to-alkane decarbonylation, which is the core of this reaction family.
